mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an imminent economic slowdown, prompting institutional investors to reduce equity exposure and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 50.0 thousand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs and compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during identified tightening phases, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS flips sign in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales volume indicates weakening consumer demand, which directly pressures near-term corporate earnings guidance and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines more than 3.0 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: The signal generates a positive predictive rank correlation over a 12-month rolling window, or forward returns following triggered events do not differ significantly from untriggered periods (bootstrap p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate paired with accelerating employment growth confirms labor market tightness, which supports wage-driven consumption and equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for FEDFUNDS, or the joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher aggregate corporate profits, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings estimates are revised upward.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 60-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05, or forward returns show no directional bias following positive GDP surprises.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by peak consumer spending, elevated retail sales volumes temporarily sustain earnings optimism, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day window before valuation mean-reversion occurs.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 60% of late-cycle instances, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 when GDP growth exceeds 3.0 percent annualized.
